description_long: This object consists of a set of four kitchen knives, each with a sleek, modern design. The blades are straight-edged and vary in length, with the longest being a chef's or carving knife and the shortest resembling a paring knife. The handles are cylindrical, black, and appear to be made of a synthetic or composite material, each featuring metallic accents near the blade junction. The blades are metallic, likely stainless steel, and have a matte or semi-gloss finish. The handles have visible rivets or fasteners, suggesting a full-tang construction for durability. There are no visible guards or elaborate ornamentation, giving the set a minimalist, functional appearance.
description: A set of four modern kitchen knives with straight metallic blades and black cylindrical handles, each featuring metallic accents and visible rivets.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length and profile of all four knives, highlighting the straight blades and cylindrical handles.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the width and shape of the blades, as well as the arrangement and thickness of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, again emphasizing the blade lengths and handle construction.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the flatness of the blades and the squared ends of the handles.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, black synthetic, metallic accents, visible rivets
